stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating its use in large language model applications.
Key technologies involved include transformers, safetensors, and the Llama architecture.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains including 'transformers' and 'text-generation'.
The mention of Meta's Llama series indicates relevance to the competitive landscape of large language models (LLMs).

stories.title: LLMs believe false statements even after explicit warnings that they're false
stories.author: Kyle Orland
bullet_points: LLMs exhibit a tendency to confidently present false statements as true, even after being warned.
Fine-tuning experiments revealed a specific bias toward asserting claims with high confidence.
This finding highlights a persistent challenge in ensuring factual accuracy and mitigating hallucination in large language models.

stories.title: GPIC: A Giant Permissive Image Corpus for Visual Generation
stories.author: Li Fei-Fei
bullet_points: Introduction of GPIC, a massive image corpus containing approximately 28 trillion pixels for visual generative modeling.
The dataset is highly accessible, featuring 100M training examples, and is permissively licensed for both research and commercial use.
GPIC is curated through safety filtering and deduplication, addressing key challenges in large-scale dataset creation.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational tasks.
It utilizes common ML components such as transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags like 'transformers' and 'pytorch'.
The pipeline indicates the model is being used for text generation tasks.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular tool for running local LLMs, now integrates MLX.
This integration significantly boosts the speed of running Ollama specifically on Apple Silicon hardware.
The feature is currently available in a preview release, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs entirely on the user's own device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI assistance for coding tasks.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily running coding tools locally or in the cloud.
It integrates various coding models such as Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification.
The models are available in two sizes (20B and 120B) and are permissively licensed under Apache 2.0.
The partnership involves ROOST (Robust Open Online Safety Tools) to enhance safety features.

stories.title: Fed up with vibe coders, dev sneaks data-nuking prompt injection into their code
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A developer intentionally introduced a prompt injection vulnerability into jqwik.
The injection instructed AI coding agents to delete application output.
This highlights security risks associated with integrating AI agents into software development workflows.

stories.title: Researchers develop a new process to get lithium out of rocks
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: Researchers have developed a novel process for extracting lithium from rocks.
The technology holds potential for diversifying global lithium supply chains.
Successful scaling of the process is key to its real-world impact.

stories.title: LLMSurgeon: Diagnosing Data Mixture of Large Language Models
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: Introduces 'Data Mixture Surgery (DMS)' to analyze the hidden composition of LLM training data.
LLMSurgeon allows researchers to estimate the domain-level distribution of an LLM's pretraining corpus using only generated text.
Addresses a critical industry gap by enabling post-hoc auditing of LLM 'digital DNA' and data provenance.

stories.title: SchGen: PCB Schematic Generation with Semantic-Grounded Code Representations
stories.author: Lili Qiu
bullet_points: SchGen is a novel LLM designed to generate editable Printed Circuit Board (PCB) schematics directly from natural language requests.
The research addresses a significant bottleneck in hardware design, moving PCB schematic generation from a manual, expertise-intensive process toward automation.
The core technical challenge addressed is developing an LLM-suited representation and acquiring a large-scale dataset for this specialized domain.

stories.title: Unlocking the Working Memory of Large Language Models for Latent Reasoning
stories.author: Sepp Hochreiter
bullet_points: Addresses a core limitation in LLM reasoning: coupling internal computation to external, autoregressive token generation.
Proposes a novel approach inspired by human cognition, utilizing internal 'working memory' for information manipulation.
Aims to decouple reasoning from external communication, potentially leading to more efficient and robust LLM reasoning capabilities.

stories.title: Locally Coherent, Globally Incoherent: Bounding Compositional Incoherence in Multi-Component LLM Agents
stories.author: Anany Kotawala
bullet_points: Addresses a critical failure mode in multi-component LLM agents where local coherence does not guarantee global correctness.
Introduces a formal measure, the 'compositional residual eps*', to quantify this 'locally coherent, globally incoherent' failure.
Provides a runtime method to compute this residual using system output and defined cross-component coupling constraints.

stories.title: LLMSurgeon: Diagnosing Data Mixture of Large Language Models
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: LLMSurgeon addresses the critical lack of transparency regarding the pretraining data composition ('digital DNA') of Large Language Models (LLMs).
The research formalizes 'Data Mixture Surgery (DMS),' a method to estimate the domain-level distribution of an LLM's training corpus using only its generated text.
This work provides a novel post-hoc auditing capability, allowing researchers to analyze and understand the underlying data influences on LLM behavior and failure modes.

stories.title: SchGen: PCB Schematic Generation with Semantic-Grounded Code Representations
stories.author: Lili Qiu
bullet_points: SchGen is a novel LLM designed to generate editable Printed Circuit Board (PCB) schematics directly from natural language requests.
The research addresses a significant gap in generative AI, as PCB schematic generation from natural language is currently unexplored.
The core technical challenge overcome involves developing an LLM-suited representation and addressing the scarcity of large-scale datasets for this domain.

stories.title: SoundnessBench: Can Your AI Scientist Really Tell Good Research Ideas from Bad Ones?
stories.author: Furong Huang
bullet_points: Introduces SoundnessBench, a new benchmark designed to test an AI's ability to judge the methodological viability of research ideas.
Addresses a critical bottleneck in autonomous AI research agents: the ability to filter poor hypotheses before resource-intensive experimentation.
The benchmark is constructed from real-world machine learning research proposals, specifically those submitted to ICLR.

stories.title: LLMSurgeon: Diagnosing Data Mixture of Large Language Models
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: LLMSurgeon addresses the lack of transparency regarding the pretraining data composition ('digital DNA') of Large Language Models (LLMs).
The research formalizes 'Data Mixture Surgery (DMS),' a method to estimate the domain-level distribution of an LLM's training corpus using only its generated text.
This work offers a novel post-hoc auditing capability to analyze and understand the underlying data influences shaping LLM behavior and failure modes.

stories.title: SchGen: PCB Schematic Generation with Semantic-Grounded Code Representations
stories.author: Lili Qiu
bullet_points: Introduces SchGen, a novel large language model capable of generating editable PCB schematics.
Addresses the significant bottleneck in hardware design: the manual and expertise-intensive nature of PCB schematic creation.
Overcomes key challenges by developing an LLM-suited representation and addressing the lack of large-scale datasets in this domain.

stories.title: Locally Coherent, Globally Incoherent: Bounding Compositional Incoherence in Multi-Component LLM Agents
stories.author: Anany Kotawala
bullet_points: Addresses a critical failure mode in multi-component LLM agents where local coherence does not guarantee global correctness.
Introduces a formal measure, the 'compositional residual eps*', to quantify this 'locally coherent, globally incoherent' failure.
Provides a runtime computable metric based on L2 distance to a joint coherent polytope, offering a potential diagnostic tool for complex LLM systems.

stories.title: MedCase-Structured: A Text-to-FHIR Dataset for Benchmarking Diagnostic Reasoning in Clinically Realistic EHR Settings
stories.author: Ziquan Fu
bullet_points: Addresses a critical gap in LLM evaluation for clinical reasoning by moving beyond static datasets.
Introduces MedCase-Structured, a new dataset pipeline that generates clinically realistic HL7 FHIR R4 bundles from unstructured text.
Aims to enable controllable and realistic benchmarking of LLMs within Electronic Health Record (EHR) environments.

stories.title: Real-time LLM Inference on Standard GPUs: 3k tokens/s per request
stories.author: NicoConstant
bullet_points: Achieves real-time LLM inference performance.
Demonstrates a throughput of 3,000 tokens per second per request.
Achieves this performance using standard, non-specialized GPUs.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is that these agents run locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is expanding its capabilities to support local image generation.
The feature is currently experimental and available on macOS.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has integrated compatibility with the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage powerful models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local model deployment and proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a working directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: This AI startup will clean your home for free to train future robots
stories.author: Robert Hart
bullet_points: AI startup Shift is offering free home cleaning services.
The service's purpose is to collect footage to train future robots.
The process involves recording cleaners performing various household tasks.

stories.title: This is MSI’s new Claw 8 EX AI Plus gaming handheld
stories.author: Jess Weatherbed
bullet_points: MSI has launched the Claw 8 EX AI Plus, a new handheld gaming PC.
The device features a specialized handheld processor, replacing previous Intel Lunar Lake chips.
MSI claims it is the 'world's first' handheld to incorporate the Intel Arc G3.

stories.title: Here's why the failure of Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket is so catastrophic
stories.author: Eric Berger
bullet_points: The article discusses the potential catastrophic failure of Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket.
The focus is on the high-stakes nature of the failure, specifically concerning potential pad damage.
This falls under the aerospace/launch vehicle industry, indicating moderate but significant industry relevance.

stories.title: The most spectacular rocket explosion since N1 just happened in Florida
stories.author: Eric Berger
bullet_points: A significant rocket explosion occurred in Florida, described as one of the most spectacular since the N1 engine failure.
The affected rocket, New Glenn, was slated to be a key component of NASA's Artemis Program.
The incident highlights risks and challenges within the commercial space launch industry.

stories.title: Physics Is All You Need? A Case Study in Physicist-Supervised AI Development of Scientific Software
stories.author: Nhat-Minh Nguyen
bullet_points: The study investigates the role of AI agents (Claude Code, Sonnet, Opus) in scientific software development, specifically in physics.
A physicist supervised the AI over 12 days to build a differentiable one-loop perturbation theory module in JAX.
The research quantifies supervision events, showing the AI autonomously resolved many tasks, supplemented by the physicist's domain expertise.

stories.title: VideoMLA: Low-Rank Latent KV Cache for Minute-Scale Autoregressive Video Diffusion
stories.author: Pinar Yanardag
bullet_points: Introduces VideoMLA, a new approach for video diffusion models.
Focuses on optimizing the latent Key-Value (KV) cache using a shared low-rank structure.
Addresses memory and latency challenges in long-rollout causal video diffusion by modifying the per-head KV layout.

stories.title: Tiny but Trusted: Efficient Vision-Language Reasoning for Time-Series Anomaly Detection
stories.author: Ismini Lourentzou
bullet_points: Addresses the limitation of current Vision-Language Models (VLMs) in detecting anomalies within sequential (time-series) data.
Highlights a key gap: existing anomaly detection benchmarks lack natural-language rationales, hindering VLM interpretability.
Introduces VisAnomBench, a new benchmark designed to facilitate the development of grounded and interpretable anomaly detection models.

stories.title: Reasoning with Sampling: Cutting at Decision Points
stories.author: Quanquan C. Liu
bullet_points: A new method, using a 'power distribution' derived from base models, can achieve comparable reasoning to models trained with reinforcement learning.
This sampling technique potentially bypasses the need for expensive post-training, curated datasets, or external verifiers.
The primary technical hurdle for practical application is developing an efficient sampler capable of mixing into this power distribution.

stories.title: DynaFLIP: Rethinking Robotics Perception via Tri-Modal-Dynamics Guided Representation
stories.author: Furong Huang
bullet_points: DynaFLIP is a new dynamics-aware multimodal pre-training framework designed to improve robot perception.
It addresses a limitation in current robotics pipelines where motion understanding is often left to downstream policies after static visual encoding.
The framework integrates image, language, and 3D flow triplets to push motion understanding upstream into the perception stage.

stories.title: Efficient Test-Time Finetuning of LLMs via Convex Reconstruction and Gradient Caching
stories.author: Alaa Maalouf
bullet_points: The paper addresses the computational bottleneck of Test-Time Finetuning (TTFT) in Large Language Models (LLMs), which is currently slow due to per-query selection and finetuning.
It introduces 'HullFT,' a novel method designed to make TTFT practical by improving efficiency.
The core technical contribution involves using 'Convex Reconstruction and Gradient Caching' to balance speed and quality in the finetuning process.

stories.title: Fairness-Aware Federated Learning with Trajectory Shapley Value
stories.author: Ziqi Wang
bullet_points: Addresses limitations in conventional Federated Learning (FL) aggregation by focusing on fairness and stability.
Introduces the Trajectory Shapley Value (TSV) to accurately account for unequal and time-varying client contributions.
Improves upon FL, a distributed paradigm designed to handle privacy-sensitive and heterogeneous data.

stories.title: Reasoning with Sampling: Cutting at Decision Points
stories.author: Quanquan C. Liu
bullet_points: The paper proposes an alternative to standard Reinforcement Learning (RL) fine-tuning for creating advanced reasoning models.
It introduces the concept of a 'power distribution' derived from the base model's distribution to elicit strong reasoning capabilities.
The main practical challenge identified is the need for an efficient sampler to effectively draw from this new power distribution.

stories.title: In-Context Reward Adaptation for Robust Preference Modeling
stories.author: Ermin Wei
bullet_points: The paper addresses a limitation in Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) where static reward models fail to capture diverse human preferences.
It proposes 'In-Context Reward Adaptation' to allow models to generalize to unseen preference domains, overcoming the limitations of fixed multi-reward frameworks.
This research aims to improve the robustness and adaptability of LLMs when aligning them with heterogeneous human values.

stories.title: Unlocking the Working Memory of Large Language Models for Latent Reasoning
stories.author: Sepp Hochreiter
bullet_points: The research addresses a limitation in current LLM reasoning by decoupling internal computation from autoregressive token generation.
It proposes using a 'working memory' mechanism, inspired by human cognition, to hold and manipulate information internally.
The goal is to enhance the reasoning capabilities of Large Language Models without relying solely on externalizing intermediate thoughts.

stories.title: COMPOSE: Composing Future Theorems from Citations and Formal Structure
stories.author: Michael Werman
bullet_points: Introduces 'COMPOSE,' a new method for generating plausible future mathematical theorems.
The model addresses a limitation in prior work by simultaneously considering both the direction of prior research and formal structural dependencies.
The goal is to create 'grounded future mathematical generation' for a given anchor paper.

stories.title: Reasoning with Sampling: Cutting at Decision Points
stories.author: Quanquan C. Liu
bullet_points: A new technique, using a 'power distribution' derived from a base language model, can achieve comparable reasoning performance to models trained with reinforcement learning.
This method bypasses the need for expensive post-training steps like RL fine-tuning, curated datasets, or external verifiers.
The primary technical challenge identified is developing an efficient sampler capable of accurately drawing samples from this specialized power distribution.

stories.title: Resolution Diagnostics for Paired LLM Evaluation
stories.author: Anany Kotawala
bullet_points: The paper addresses a critical issue in LLM evaluation: many existing pairwise rankings on leaderboards fail to meet conventional resolution targets.
Specific findings show that 11 of 40 Open LLM Leaderboard v1 comparisons and 4 of 9 MMLU-Pro adjacent-rank pairs are unresolved under standard paired evaluation design.
The research frames paired LLM evaluation as a hypothesis testing problem, suggesting a need for more robust diagnostic methods.

stories.title: Why 99% of RAG Apps Crash in Production (Naive vs Scaled Node.js)
stories.author: gaurav_thorat_669a72b30ba
bullet_points: Addresses a common production failure point for Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) applications.
Compares naive implementation practices against scalable Node.js architectures in a real-world context.
Highlights critical production issues like socket exhaustion, rate limiting (429s), and connection pool timeouts.

stories.title: How Model Distillation Actually Works (and What the 'China Distilled Our Model' Headlines Really Mean)
stories.author: p0rt
bullet_points: Provides a practical, low-hype explanation of the mechanics behind knowledge distillation in Large Language Models (LLMs).
Differentiates the technical implications of distilling knowledge from closed, proprietary APIs versus open models.
Analyzes the context and meaning behind recent industry claims regarding model distillation (e.g., OpenAI/Anthropic vs. DeepSeek allegations).

stories.title: Executable Architectural Intent: The Promotion Path From Docs to Constraints
stories.author: mnemehq
bullet_points: The article discusses the concept of promoting project knowledge from human-readable documentation into machine-evaluable constraints.
This process directly impacts and binds the behavior of AI agents during the generation phase of software or systems.
It represents a shift towards making architectural intent explicit and executable rather than purely descriptive.
